summary: qv8 a11 regressed from a clean abstention in the prior top8 baseline to a non-abstaining, candidate-style answer in the new full top5 run. The retrieval stayed on the right issuer, but the answer shape lost the refusal gate and expanded into a long evaluation-rule explanation instead of stopping at not-in-docs.

models.review_method: Manual comparison of saved details.json evidence only; no model calls or external APIs were used.

outputs.description: proposal contract comparing qv8 a11 baseline/top8 versus full top5 abstention-boundary behavior

case_under_review.expected_behavior: Refuse to invent the final award score or selected vendor because the provided documents only support evaluation rules, not the final award result.

baseline_top8.answer_shape: Refusal-first answer with a short evidence section after the refusal.
baseline_top8.retrieval_context_note: Anchored to the correct issuer, but still stayed inside the abstention boundary.

full_top5.answer_shape: Starts with issuer/title framing and then turns into evaluation criteria, scoring rules, and calculation guidance instead of refusing.
full_top5.retrieval_context_note: Still on the correct issuer, but the model used the context to answer rather than to abstain.

before_after_quality_comparison.net_assessment: This is an answer-boundary failure, not a retrieval miss. The top5 run skipped the refusal prefix and spent its extra completion budget on a long rule explanation.

failure_breakdown.answer_shape: Baseline top8 uses a refusal-first shape. Full top5 opens with business name and issuer, then pivots into numbered evidence and calculation instructions. That shape reads like a partial answer, not an abstention.
failure_breakdown.refusal_prefix: Baseline top8 has an explicit refusal lead. Full top5 has no refusal prefix at all, so the boundary gate never fired.
failure_breakdown.detail_tail: The top5 answer spends its body on award-evaluation rules, tie-break logic, and required inputs for calculation. Those details are precisely why the answer should have stayed short and refused.
failure_breakdown.retrieved_context: Both runs retrieved the right issuer, and the top5 run had a smaller retrieved set (5 docs instead of 8). That likely reduced guardrail pressure while still leaving enough rule text for the model to over-answer.
failure_breakdown.generation_tokens: Top5 used fewer prompt tokens but more completion tokens: 2741 prompt / 1681 completion / 4422 total versus 4173 prompt / 1225 completion / 5398 total in the baseline. The extra completion budget was spent on explanation rather than refusal.

proposed_failure_label.label: non_abstaining_award_result_with_long_rule_explanation
proposed_failure_label.definition: The answer should have refused because the award result is not in the documents, but instead it provides a long rule-based explanation, calculation guidance, or candidate-style evidence summary without a refusal gate.

detector_and_scoring_rule.name: award_result_refusal_gate_with_detail_tail_penalty
detector_and_scoring_rule.scope: Apply when expect_abstention is true for unsupported award-result questions.
trigger_conditions: answer is not classified as abstention
the query asks for a final score, winner, selection result, or equivalent award outcome
the documents only support evaluation rules or inputs, not the final result
the answer contains long rule summaries, scoring formulas, or calculation instructions instead of a refusal
scoring_effect: Mark the case as a hard abstention failure when the refusal prefix is missing.
Treat a long rule explanation as evidence of boundary violation, not as acceptable helpful context.
Do not reward detailed calculation instructions unless the user explicitly asked for derivation after supplying all required inputs.
detector_and_scoring_rule.minimal_rule: If expect_abstention is true and the answer lacks an explicit refusal phrase but includes evaluation rules, scoring formulae, or follow-up calculation guidance, classify it as a boundary failure even if the retrieved documents are relevant.

prompt_change_suggestion.suggested_rule: For unsupported award-result questions, answer in one sentence: say the final score or winner is not in the provided documents, then stop. Do not add scoring formulas, tie-break rules, or next-step calculation guidance unless the user explicitly asks for those inputs.
prompt_change_suggestion.why_narrow: This preserves helpfulness for genuine calculation requests while preventing the model from drifting into a quasi-answer when the award result itself is unavailable.

commands_run: Select-String q20260707_a11_dbrain_award_score_and_vendor with context from both evidence files
observed_results: baseline_top8 abstention=true with an explicit refusal and 11 lines of answer text
full_top5 answer begins with issuer/title framing and expands into evaluation rules instead of refusing
top5 completion_tokens=1681 versus baseline completion_tokens=1225, showing the extra generation budget was spent on explanation
retrieval coverage remained aligned to the same issuer in both runs, so the defect is answer shaping rather than retrieval failure

commands_run: Select-String q20260707_a11_dbrain_award_score_and_vendor with context from both evidence files
observed_results: baseline_top8 q11: abstention=true, expect_abstention=true, answer_chars=641, answer_line_count=11, post_refusal_tail_chars=570, post_refusal_bullet_count=8, prompt_tokens=4173, completion_tokens=1225, total_tokens=5398

full_top5 q11: abstention=false, expect_abstention=true, answer_chars=722, answer_line_count=14, post_refusal_tail_chars=0, post_refusal_bullet_count=0, prompt_tokens=2741, completion_tokens=1681, total_tokens=4422
baseline_top8 retrieved 8 docs, full_top5 retrieved 5 docs, and both were anchored to the same issuer
